# Domphy

> UI as plain JS objects — no JSX, no compiler, no virtual DOM. `@domphy/core` handles reactivity, rendering, CSS-in-JS, and SSR. `@domphy/ui` ships 85 patches that apply behavior directly to native HTML elements. **No wrappers, no component hierarchy.** Fits tool apps well (plugins, extensions, dashboards) — drop it in a script tag and ship. Built to be **learnable by AI in-context** (this file) and **self-correcting** via `@domphy/doctor`, so agents write correct Domphy despite thin training data.

A **patch** is a function returning a `PartialElement`: a stateless descriptor applied to a host element via the `$` property. Patches carry no rendering lifecycle, own no state, and create no DOM boundary. Compose by listing multiple patches; the runtime merges them on the native element.

## Why this is the AI-friendly framework

- **One spec, in context.** The whole API is learnable from this file (`llms.txt`) or `AGENTS.md` — no large training corpus needed.
- **Self-correcting.** `@domphy/doctor` (`diagnose`/`validate`) flags non-idiomatic output; feed the report back and the model fixes itself.
- **Machine-readable.** `manifest.json` indexes every package + patch (host tag, props schema, example); `tones.json` lists valid tones + theme colors; the `@domphy/mcp` server exposes patches, packages, rules, tones, the doctor (incl. `domphy_fix`), AND your app's own blocks as tools.
- **Scales to large codebases.** An app-block registry (`app-manifest.json`) + the MCP `domphy_list_app_blocks`/`domphy_get_app_block` tools let an agent discover the blocks an app already has instead of re-creating them.

## Rules for code generation

- Build UIs as plain objects keyed by HTML tag (e.g. `{ button: "Click", $: [button()] }`).
- Apply patches via `$`. Never wrap a native tag in a component abstraction.
- **Never inline typography styles** — `fontSize`, `fontWeight`, `lineHeight`, `letterSpacing`, `fontFamily`, `textDecoration`, `color` in `style:` are ALL forbidden. Use patches instead:

  | You want | Use patch | Example |
  |---|---|---|
  | small / secondary / caption / label / helper text | `small()` | `{ span: "hint", $: [small()] }` |
  | body / paragraph text | `paragraph()` | `{ p: "body copy", $: [paragraph()] }` |
  | heading / title | `heading()` | `{ h2: "Title", $: [heading()] }` |
  | bold / emphasis | `strong()` | `{ strong: "bold", $: [strong()] }` |
  | inline code | `code()` | `{ code: "fn()", $: [code()] }` |
  | link | `link()` | `{ a: "click", href: "...", $: [link()] }` |
  | colored text (error/warn/etc) | patch `color` prop | `{ span: "err", $: [small({ color: "error" })] }` |
  | any literal hex/rgb color | `themeColor()` | `color: (l) => themeColor(l, "base", "error")` |

  Remove `fontFamily` entirely — the theme owns the font stack.
- **Forms use `@domphy/form`** — `createForm` from `@domphy/form/domphy`; bind inputs with `value: (l) => field.value(l)` + `onInput: (e) => field.handleChange(e.target.value)`. The old ui `form()`/`field()` patches and `FormState`/`FieldState` were REMOVED; only the layout patch `formGroup()` remains in `@domphy/ui`.
- Reactive content/attributes use `(listener) => state.get(listener)`. A controlled input (value bound to a state you also `.set()` in `onInput`) is safe. For derived values prefer `computed(fn)` (lazy + cached); side-effects use `effect(fn)`; watch old+new values with `watch(source, (newVal, oldVal) => {})`; group disposal with `effectScope()`; coalesce writes with `batch(fn)`. In tests/imperative code, `flushSync()` drains pending reactivity synchronously so the DOM reflects a `.set()` immediately. Await the next reactive flush asynchronously with `nextTick()`. Type-guard reactive sources with `isState(v)`. Expose read-only state with `readonly(state)` (hides `.set()`).
- **Data/logic packages are 1-1 TanStack core ports + a Domphy adapter at the `/domphy` subpath** (`@domphy/core` is a peer dep): `createQuery`/`createMutation`/`createInfiniteQuery` (`@domphy/query/domphy`), `createDomphyTable` (`@domphy/table/domphy`), `createVirtualizer` (`@domphy/virtual/domphy`), `createForm` (`@domphy/form/domphy`), `createRouter`/`createRoute`/`createRootRoute`/`createRootRouteWithContext` (`@domphy/router`). Drag & drop: `dragDrop()` / `multiList()` / `multiListGroup()` (`@domphy/dnd`). Animation: the `motion()` patch (`@domphy/ui`). Next-style app framework: `@domphy/app`.
- **Self-check generated code with `@domphy/doctor`**: `diagnose(element, opts?)` / `validate(element, opts?)` + `format(...)` reports `inline-typography` (fontSize/lineHeight/fontWeight/letterSpacing/fontFamily/textDecoration literals — use patches) / `raw-theme-value` (literal hex/rgb colors AND CSS named colors like "red"/"white"/"black" on direct color props — hint gives nearest `themeColor()` via CIELAB/LCH match powered by `@domphy/palette`) / `raw-spacing-value` (literal rem/em/px on any margin/padding/gap property — use `themeSpacing(n)`) / `unknown-tone` (invalid dataTone; valid: inherit/base/shift-N/increase-N/decrease-N, N ≤ 17) / `middle-surface-anchor` (dataTone shift-4–13 mid-ramp collapses contrast — use edge anchors shift-0–3 or shift-14–17) / `unknown-density` (invalid dataDensity; valid: inherit/increase-N/decrease-N, N ≤ 4) / `unknown-size` (invalid dataSize; valid: inherit/increase-N/decrease-N, N ≤ 7) / `void-content` / `missing-key` / `duplicate-key` / `unstable-key` / `unknown-tag` — fix what it lists before finishing (`fix(element)` auto-applies the lossless ones). `DiagnoseOptions`: `runReactive` (default true), `only: string[]` (whitelist rules), `exclude: string[]` (blacklist rules), `rules: CustomRule[]` (project-specific rules). Inline suppression: `_doctorDisable: true | string | string[]` on an element suppresses rules at that node. `Diagnostic.category`: "structure" | "key" | "theme" | "typography" | "data-attr".
- Read the per-patch / per-package doc before using one you haven't used before. Each has its own prop contract.

## Getting started

- Scaffold a project: `npm create domphy@latest <dir>` — a runnable Vite + TS starter (themeApply + sample patches + AGENTS.md).
- [5-minute quickstart](https://domphy.com/docs/quickstart): install, hello world, patches, reactive state, forms
- [Full docs index](https://domphy.com/docs/): every package
- [Building with AI](https://domphy.com/docs/ai): per-tool setup (Cursor, Copilot, and any AI editor)
- [Why Domphy](https://domphy.com/docs/guide/why-domphy): comparison vs React/Svelte/Solid/Vue, bundle sizes, when to use
- [Coming from React](https://domphy.com/docs/guide/from-react): React concepts → Domphy equivalents (useState→toState, hooks→lifecycle, components→patches, React Query→@domphy/query, etc.)

## Core runtime (`@domphy/core`)

- [Syntax](https://domphy.com/docs/core/syntax): object shape, `$`, content, event handlers, attribute keys
- [Reactivity](https://domphy.com/docs/core/reactivity): `toState`, `state.get(listener)`, `RecordState`, `computed`, `effect`, `effectScope`, `batch`, `untrack`, `flushSync`, `watch`, `nextTick`, `isState`, `readonly`; types: `ReadableState<T>`, `ValueOrState<T>`, `Computed<T>`, `WatchSource<T>`, `WatchOptions` (`computed()` satisfies `ReadableState<T>`, so a `Computed<T>` is accepted anywhere `ValueOrState<T>` is; `toState()` also accepts `ReadableState<T>`; `watch(source, (newVal, oldVal) => {})` — tracks a State/Computed/getter, gives old+new value; `nextTick()` — async wait after reactive flush; `isState(v)` — type guard for State/ReadableState; `readonly(state)` — exposes `.get()` only, no `.set()`)
- [Lifecycle](https://domphy.com/docs/core/lifecycle): `_onSchedule`, `_onInit`, `_onInsert`, `_onMount`, `_onBeforeUpdate`, `_onUpdate`, `_onBeforeRemove`, `_onRemove`, `_onError` (error boundary — nearest ancestor with `_onError` catches errors thrown by reactive children; call `reset()` to clear children and render fallback)
- [Context & Shared State](https://domphy.com/docs/core/context): module-level state as context, event bus, scoped context objects
- [Refs & DOM Access](https://domphy.com/docs/core/refs): `_onMount`/`_onRemove` lifecycle, closing over DOM refs, third-party library wiring
- [Error Handling](https://domphy.com/docs/core/error-handling): `_onError` boundary, async error patterns, global error handlers
- [Performance](https://domphy.com/docs/core/performance): granular subscriptions, `_key`, computed memoization, `batch()`, lazy rendering
- [Common Patterns](https://domphy.com/docs/core/patterns): state machines, reducers, compound components, factories, code organization
- [Composing Elements](https://domphy.com/docs/core/composing): component functions, slots, merging, reactive patterns
- [Migration Guide](https://domphy.com/docs/core/migration): v0.17→v0.18 breaking changes, form patch removal, themeColor signature
- [TypeScript](https://domphy.com/docs/core/typescript): `DomphyElement`, `State<T>`, event handler types, component patterns
- [Testing](https://domphy.com/docs/core/testing): Vitest+jsdom setup, testing reactive state, ElementNode in tests
- [Portal](https://domphy.com/docs/core/portal): render into arbitrary containers
- [SSR](https://domphy.com/docs/core/ssr): server rendering + hydration model
- [Config](https://domphy.com/docs/core/api/utilities): `configure({ cspNonce })` — stamp a CSP nonce on all Domphy-injected `<style>` elements; call before mounting.

## Theme (`@domphy/theme`)

- [Setup](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/setup): install and mount the theme
- [Palette](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/palette): `themeColor`, shift scales
- [Color Roles & Semantic](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/color-roles): surface/text/border roles, semantic families, interactive states, `themeColorToken`
- [Dark Mode](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/dark-mode): `data-theme` switching, SSR-safe init, system preference
- [Spacing System](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/spacing): `themeSpacing(n)`, density multiplier, layout vs component
- [Typography](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/typography): type scale, font tokens, responsive text
- [Motion & Animation](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/motion): CSS transitions, keyframes, reduced motion
- [Responsive Design](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/responsive): breakpoints, container queries, adaptive layouts
- [Size](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/size): `themeSize`, `themeSpacing`, density
- [Tone](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/tone): light/dark handling
- [API](https://domphy.com/docs/theme/api): full theme API reference

### Theme quick-reference

**Color** — `themeColor(object, tone?, color?)`:
- `tone`: `"inherit"` | `"base"` | `"shift-N"` | `"increase-N"` | `"decrease-N"` (N ≤ 17, 18-step ramp)
- `color`: built-in families: `"neutral"` | `"primary"` | `"secondary"` | `"info"` | `"success"` | `"warning"` | `"attention"` | `"error"` | `"danger"` | `"highlight"` — or any custom family registered via `setTheme()`; default `"inherit"`
- Surface roles from edge anchor: bg=`"inherit"`, stroke=`"shift-3"`, muted=`"shift-6"`, body text=`"shift-9"`, heading=`"shift-11"`, hover=`"increase-1"`, pressed=`"increase-2"`
- **dataTone**: edge anchors `shift-0`–`shift-3` (light) or `shift-14`–`shift-17` (dark). Mid-ramp `shift-4`–`shift-13` → `middle-surface-anchor` warning (collapses contrast).
- `themeColorToken(object, tone?, color?)` — same signature as `themeColor` but returns the resolved token value (e.g. `"#4a7ff4"`) instead of a `var(--…)` CSS reference. Use at design-time or when a third-party API requires a concrete hex/rgb string.

**Spacing** — `themeSpacing(n)` returns `"calc(n/4 em)"` as a CSS string (e.g. `themeSpacing(4)` → `"calc(1em)"`). Always wrapped in `calc()` for composability:
- For bounded controls: `themeSpacing(themeDensity(l) * n)` — density scales padding/gap.
- For structural layout: bare `themeSpacing(n)` (no density multiplier).
- Never hardcode `"6px"` / `"1.5em"` — doctor flags as `raw-spacing-value`.
- **dataDensity**: `"inherit"` | `"increase-N"` | `"decrease-N"` where N ≤ 4 (5-step scale: 0.75, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5).

**Size** — `themeSize(listener, size?)`:
- `size`: `"inherit"` | `"increase-N"` | `"decrease-N"` (N ≤ 7, 8-step scale)
- Never hardcode `fontSize: "16px"` — use `fontSize: (l) => themeSize(l, "inherit")`.
- **dataSize**: `"inherit"` | `"increase-N"` | `"decrease-N"` where N ≤ 7.

**Bounded control geometry formula** (button/input, density `d`, 1 line):
```ts
paddingBlock: (l) => themeSpacing(themeDensity(l) * 1),
paddingInline: (l) => themeSpacing(themeDensity(l) * 3),
borderRadius: (l) => themeSpacing(themeDensity(l) * 1),
// Use outline not border — border adds 2px to height, deviating from canonical sizes.
```

**Theme mount & palette API** (`themeApply` / `themeCSS` / `setTheme` / `getTheme` / `themeTokens` / `themeVars` / `themeName` / `applySystemTheme` / `themeFluidSpacing`):
- `themeApply(el?)` — writes all registered palette CSS into `<style id="domphy-themes">` (or into `el` if supplied). Call once before mounting.
- `themeCSS()` — returns the full palette CSS string without touching the DOM (useful for SSR `<style>` injection).
- `setTheme(name, input)` — registers or deep-merges a named color palette into the theme registry; clears var/token caches. `colors` may include brand-new family names alongside built-in ones — new families auto-generate CSS vars and work with `themeColor()` immediately.
- `getTheme(name)` — returns the raw `ThemeInput` record for a named theme.
- `themeTokens(name)` — returns resolved concrete values (hex strings, font sizes) for a named theme — useful for programmatic inspection or passing to third-party APIs.
- `themeVars()` — returns `ThemeVars`: a parallel structure of `var(--…)` references (used internally by `themeColor`).
- `themeName(listenerOrNode)` — walks the element tree from a listener/`ElementNode` to find the nearest `data-theme` ancestor; subscribes the listener to changes. Returns `"light"` if none found.
- `applySystemTheme(targetEl?, options?)` — reads `localStorage` preference first, falls back to `prefers-color-scheme`, sets `data-theme` on `targetEl` (default: `document.documentElement`), and listens for OS changes. Returns a cleanup fn. Options: `persist` (boolean, default `true`), `storageKey` (string, default `"dp-theme"`). **Call once on client startup instead of writing the matchMedia boilerplate manually.** SSR-safe: guard with `typeof window !== "undefined"`.
- `themeFluidSpacing(min, max, viewportMin?, viewportMax?)` — returns a CSS `clamp()` that scales between `themeSpacing(min)` and `themeSpacing(max)` across the viewport range (default 320 px → 1280 px). Use for page padding and section gaps; **not** for bounded-control padding (use density there).

## Patches (`@domphy/ui`)

### Typography
- [small](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/small), [paragraph](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/paragraph), [heading](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/heading), [blockquote](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/blockquote), [strong](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/strong), [emphasis](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/emphasis), [mark](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/mark), [code](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/code), [keyboard](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/keyboard), [preformated](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/preformated), [link](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/link), [abbreviation](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/abbreviation), [subscript](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/subscript), [superscript](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/superscript), [label](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/label)

### Controls
- [button](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/button), [button-ghost](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/button-ghost), [button-switch](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/button-switch), [toggle-group](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/toggle-group) (all-in-one `items` API, single or multi-select), [segmented](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/segmented) (all-in-one `items` API), [date-picker](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/date-picker), [input-text](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-text), [input-number](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-number), [input-search](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-search), [input-checkbox](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-checkbox), [input-radio](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-radio), [input-switch](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-switch), [input-range](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-range), [input-color](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-color), [input-date-time](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-date-time), [input-file](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-file), [input-otp](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-otp), [input-password](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/input-password) (styled `<div>` with inner `<input type="password">` + show/hide toggle), [textarea](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/textarea), [select](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/select), [select-box](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/select-box), [select-item](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/select-item), [select-list](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/select-list), [combobox](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/combobox)

### Forms
- [form-group](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/form-group) (layout only). Form state/validation lives in `@domphy/form` (see below), not in `@domphy/ui`.

### Animation
- [motion](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/motion) (declarative `animate`/`initial`/`exit` via the Web Animations API), [transition-group](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/transition-group) (reorder/FLIP)

### Feedback
- [alert](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/alert), [toast](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/toast), [badge](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/badge), [tag](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/tag), [progress](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/progress), [ring-progress](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/ring-progress) (circular donut via CSS conic-gradient + mask), [spinner](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/spinner), [skeleton](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/skeleton), [tooltip](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/tooltip), [steps](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/steps) (all-in-one `items` + `current` API), [error-boundary](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/error-boundary), [rating](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/rating)
- Custom spinner variants: use [svg-spinners](https://github.com/n3r4zzurr0/svg-spinners) (MIT, 28+ styles). Convert SVG to Domphy elements — `fill="currentColor"` on root svg, CSS-in-JS `@keyframes` with `hashString` for unique name, `style.color: themeColor()`. No innerHTML. See [spinner docs](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/spinner).

### Overlays
- [dialog](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/dialog), [drawer](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/drawer) (`"start"`/`"end"` placements resolve to left/right based on document `dir` for RTL support), [popover](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/popover), [popover-arrow](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/popover-arrow), [menu](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/menu) (all-in-one `items` API; `items: []` keeps the wrapper's own children), [command](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/command) (with its `commandItem` / `commandSearch` sub-patches)

### Layout
- [accordion](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/accordion), [card](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/card), [divider](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/divider), [horizontal-rule](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/horizontal-rule), [scroll-area](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/scroll-area) (themed overlay scrollbar via `::-webkit-scrollbar` + `scrollbar-width`), [splitter](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/splitter) (+ `splitterPanel()` / `splitterHandle()`), [list](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/list) (+ `listItem()` / `listItemButton()`), [timeline](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/timeline) (+ `timelineItem()`), [fab](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/fab), [figure](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/figure), [tabs](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/tabs) (all-in-one `items` API — generates tablist + panels), [details](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/details), [breadcrumb](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/breadcrumb), [breadcrumb-ellipsis](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/breadcrumb-ellipsis), [pagination](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/pagination), [toolbar](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/toolbar) (+ `toolbarSpacer()`), [empty](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/empty)

### Data
- [table](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/table), [ordered-list](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/ordered-list), [unordered-list](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/unordered-list), [description-list](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/description-list), [avatar](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/avatar), [image](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/image), [icon](https://domphy.com/docs/ui/patches/icon)

## Query (`@domphy/query`)

Async state management — a 1-1 port of `@tanstack/query-core` (identical API). Adapter at `@domphy/query/domphy`: `createQuery`/`createMutation`/`createInfiniteQuery` + `bindResult` (binds a query/mutation observer to Domphy reactivity so result fields are readable with a listener); types: `QueryHandle`, `InfiniteQueryHandle`, `MutationHandle`, `ReactiveResult`.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/query/) · [Queries](https://domphy.com/docs/query/queries) · [Mutations](https://domphy.com/docs/query/mutations) · [Parallel & Dependent](https://domphy.com/docs/query/parallel) · [Caching](https://domphy.com/docs/query/caching) · [Infinite Queries](https://domphy.com/docs/query/infinite-queries) · [Polling & Window Focus](https://domphy.com/docs/query/polling) · [Query Invalidation](https://domphy.com/docs/query/invalidation) · [Testing](https://domphy.com/docs/query/testing) · [Suspense & Streaming](https://domphy.com/docs/query/suspense) · [SSR](https://domphy.com/docs/query/ssr) · [Advanced Patterns](https://domphy.com/docs/query/advanced) · [TypeScript](https://domphy.com/docs/query/typescript) · [Devtools](https://domphy.com/docs/query/devtools) · [API](https://domphy.com/docs/query/api)

## Router (`@domphy/router`)

Type-safe routing — a 1-1 port of `@tanstack/router-core` (identical API) plus `createRouter`/`createRoute`/`createRootRoute`/`createRootRouteWithContext`.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/router/) · [Routes](https://domphy.com/docs/router/routes) · [Navigation](https://domphy.com/docs/router/navigation) · [Search Params](https://domphy.com/docs/router/search-params) · [Data Loading](https://domphy.com/docs/router/data-loading) · [Data Mutations](https://domphy.com/docs/router/data-mutations) · [Code Splitting](https://domphy.com/docs/router/lazy-loading) · [Authentication](https://domphy.com/docs/router/authentication) · [Error Handling](https://domphy.com/docs/router/error-handling) · [Navigation Blocking](https://domphy.com/docs/router/navigation-blocking) · [Router Context](https://domphy.com/docs/router/router-context) · [Type Safety](https://domphy.com/docs/router/type-safety) · [Scroll Restoration](https://domphy.com/docs/router/scroll-restoration) · [SSR](https://domphy.com/docs/router/ssr) · [API](https://domphy.com/docs/router/api)

## Table (`@domphy/table`)

Headless table logic — a 1-1 port of `@tanstack/table-core` v8 (identical API). Adapter `createDomphyTable` (`@domphy/table/domphy`).

**`DomphyTable` handle** (returned by `createDomphyTable`):
- `table` — raw table-core `Table` instance (all feature methods: `setSorting`, `setColumnFilters`, `setGlobalFilter`, `nextPage`, `toggleAllRowsSelected`, `setColumnVisibility`, `toggleAllColumnsVisible`, `setColumnOrder`, `setGrouping`, `toggleAllRowsExpanded`, etc.)
- `version(l?)` — reactive change counter; bumps on every state change
- `state(l?)` — full `TableState`, reactive with listener
- `setState(updater)` — direct state update
- `destroy()` — releases reactive state
- **Reactive reads** (accept optional listener `l`): `getRowModel(l?)`, `getHeaderGroups(l?)`, `getAllLeafColumns(l?)`, `getVisibleLeafColumns(l?)`, `getIsAllColumnsVisible(l?)`, `getIsSomeColumnsVisible(l?)`, `getSelectedRowModel(l?)`, `getIsAllRowsSelected(l?)`, `getIsSomeRowsSelected(l?)`, `getIsAllRowsExpanded(l?)`, `getCanNextPage(l?)`, `getCanPreviousPage(l?)`, `getPageCount(l?)`

Two render patterns: (1) coarse — call `version(l)` once, then call `table.getRowModel()` etc. without listener; (2) fine — pass listener directly to a convenience read method (`dTable.getCanNextPage(l)`) so only that element re-renders.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/table/) · [Columns & Row Models](https://domphy.com/docs/table/columns) · [Sorting & Filtering](https://domphy.com/docs/table/sorting-filtering) · [Pagination & Selection](https://domphy.com/docs/table/pagination-selection) · [Advanced](https://domphy.com/docs/table/advanced) · [Column Groups & Headers](https://domphy.com/docs/table/column-groups) · [Grouping & Expanding](https://domphy.com/docs/table/grouping) · [Column Visibility & Ordering](https://domphy.com/docs/table/column-visibility) · [Faceting & Filter UI](https://domphy.com/docs/table/faceting) · [Row Pinning](https://domphy.com/docs/table/row-pinning) · [Custom Features](https://domphy.com/docs/table/custom-features) · [Virtualization](https://domphy.com/docs/table/virtualization) · [API](https://domphy.com/docs/table/api)

## Virtual (`@domphy/virtual`)

List/grid virtualization — a 1-1 port of `@tanstack/virtual-core` (identical API). Adapter `createVirtualizer` (`@domphy/virtual/domphy`).

**`VirtualizerHandle`** (returned by `createVirtualizer`):
- `getVirtualItems(l?)` — reactive list of visible `VirtualItem`s
- `getTotalSize(l?)` — reactive total scroll size for the spacer
- `setScrollElement(el)` — wire the scroll container from `_onMount`
- `measureElement(el)` — dynamic measurement ref; call from item `_onMount`
- `scrollToIndex(i, opts?)` / `scrollToOffset(px, opts?)` — absolute scroll
- `scrollBy(delta, opts?)` — relative scroll from current position
- `scrollToEnd(opts?)` — scroll to last item
- `setOptions(opts)` — update `count`/options and re-measure
- `version(l?)` — raw reactive change counter
- `destroy()` — detach observers; call from `_onRemove`
- `virtualizer` — underlying `Virtualizer` instance; exposes `takeSnapshot()`, `getDistanceFromEnd()`, `isAtEnd(threshold?)`, `resizeItem(index, size)`, `measure()`

Key options: `count`, `estimateSize`, `horizontal`, `overscan`, `gap`, `paddingStart`, `paddingEnd`, `lanes`, `laneAssignmentMode` (`"estimate"|"measured"`), `anchorTo`, `followOnAppend`, `getItemKey`, `rangeExtractor`, `scrollMargin`, `initialMeasurementsCache`, `initialOffset`, `useCachedMeasurements`, `enabled`, `isRtl`.

Extras vs upstream: iOS WebKit momentum-scroll deferral, `scrollBy`/`scrollToEnd`/`takeSnapshot`/`getDistanceFromEnd`/`isAtEnd` directly on the handle, lazy typed-array fast-path (100 k-item cold mount in <1 ms), `laneAssignmentMode`/`useCachedMeasurements`.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/) · [Usage Patterns](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/usage) · [Infinite Scroll](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/infinite-scroll) · [Grid Virtualization](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/grid) · [API Reference](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/api) · [Scroll Navigation](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/scroll-to) · [Dynamic Sizes](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/dynamic-sizes) · [Masonry Layout](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/masonry) · [Window Virtualizer](https://domphy.com/docs/virtual/window-virtualizer)

## Form (`@domphy/form`)

Headless form state/validation — a 1-1 port of `@tanstack/form-core` (identical API). Adapter `createForm` (`@domphy/form/domphy`); `form.field(name, opts)` returns a reactive field handle.

**`FormHandle` reactive reads** (pass listener `l`): `form.values(l)`, `form.state(l)`, `form.canSubmit(l)`, `form.isSubmitting(l)`, `form.isValid(l)`, `form.isSubmitted(l)`, `form.isDirty(l)`, `form.isPristine(l)`, `form.isTouched(l)`, `form.isBlurred(l)`.
**`FormHandle` imperative**: `form.getFieldValue(field)`, `form.setFieldValue(field, updater)`, `form.validateField(field, cause?)`, `form.reset(values?)`, `form.handleSubmit()`, `form.destroy()`. Raw core: `form.form` (FormApi).
**`FieldHandle` reactive**: `field.value(l)`, `field.errors(l)`, `field.meta(l)`. **Imperative**: `field.handleChange(v)`, `field.handleBlur()`, `field.setValue(v)`, `field.validate(cause?)`. **Array helpers**: `field.pushValue(item)`, `field.insertValue(index, item)`, `field.replaceValue(index, item)`, `field.removeValue(index)`, `field.swapValues(a, b)`, `field.moveValue(a, b)`, `field.clearValues()`. Raw core: `field.api` (FieldApi).

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/form/) · [Core Concepts](https://domphy.com/docs/form/concepts) · [Validation](https://domphy.com/docs/form/validation) · [Field Arrays](https://domphy.com/docs/form/arrays) · [Submission & Async](https://domphy.com/docs/form/submission) · [Listeners & Side Effects](https://domphy.com/docs/form/listeners) · [Async Initial Values](https://domphy.com/docs/form/async-values) · [TypeScript](https://domphy.com/docs/form/typescript) · [UI Patches & Form Fields](https://domphy.com/docs/form/ui-integration) · [Devtools](https://domphy.com/docs/form/devtools) · [Form Composition](https://domphy.com/docs/form/composition) · [Testing Forms](https://domphy.com/docs/form/testing) · [SSR & Hydration](https://domphy.com/docs/form/ssr) · [Linked Fields](https://domphy.com/docs/form/linked-fields)

## DnD (`@domphy/dnd`)

Drag & drop / sortable lists — wraps the framework-agnostic `@formkit/drag-and-drop` engine.

**Adapters (Domphy-specific):**
- `dragDrop(state, config?)` — single sortable list; animations enabled by default (`animated: false` to opt out). Pass `group: "name"` for cross-list transfer, `dragHandle: ".cls"` for custom handles.
- `multiList({ group, values, config? })` — one list in a multi-container group. All lists with the same `group` string accept transfers from each other.
- `multiListGroup(group, [stateA, stateB, ...], config?)` — shorthand; returns `[dropA, dropB, ...]` to zip with containers.

**Re-exports everything from `@formkit/drag-and-drop`:** `animations()`, `insert()`, `dropOrSwap()` plugins; `updateConfig()`, `tearDown()`; all event/config types.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/dnd/) · [API Reference](https://domphy.com/docs/dnd/api) · [Sortable List](https://domphy.com/docs/dnd/sortable) · [Multi-Container](https://domphy.com/docs/dnd/multi-container) · [Multi-Drag](https://domphy.com/docs/dnd/multi-drag) · [Animations](https://domphy.com/docs/dnd/animations) · [Handles & Filtering](https://domphy.com/docs/dnd/handles-and-filtering) · [Accessibility](https://domphy.com/docs/dnd/accessibility)

## Blocks (`@domphy/blocks`)

173 composed blocks and effect components — sidebar layouts, auth pages, a dashboard, `@domphy/chart` recipes (from shadcn/ui's public blocks + chart galleries), and marketing/effect components (from Magic UI's public catalog). Every export is a **factory function** `name(props?) => DomphyElement` returning a complete mountable tree — not a `$`-patch merged onto a caller's tag. `props` is optional with sane defaults, so `name()` alone renders a working demo; only genuinely variable parts (data/items/labels/colors) are parameterized.

**Clean-room implementation:** each component was specified (behavior/visual description only, from the public reference) by one pass and implemented by a separate pass that never saw the original source or website — no third-party code is copied or redistributed. See `packages/blocks/SOURCES.md` for the full manifest (export, file, reference URL, status, fidelity notes) and `pnpm --filter @domphy/blocks visual-compare` for an on-demand Playwright screenshot comparison against the public reference.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/blocks/) · [shadcn/ui](https://domphy.com/docs/blocks/shadcn) · [Magic UI](https://domphy.com/docs/blocks/magicui) · [Methodology](https://domphy.com/docs/blocks/methodology) · [API](https://domphy.com/docs/blocks/api)

## App (`@domphy/app`)

Next.js App Router-style framework — runtime (no compiler, no file-system routing): nested routes/layouts, loaders with stale-while-revalidate, metadata, middleware, parallel + intercepting routes, **lazy / code-split routes** (`lazy: () => import(...)`), SSR + streaming (`renderToString`/`renderToStream`), API routes, **i18n routing** (`createI18nMiddleware` + `getLocale`), **cookie parsing** (`cookies(headers?)`). Routes are a plain object tree via `defineRoutes`; `createApp(routes)`. (This docs site is built on it — see Markdown below.)

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/app/) · [Routing](https://domphy.com/docs/app/routing) · [Layouts & Boundaries](https://domphy.com/docs/app/layouts) · [Navigation](https://domphy.com/docs/app/navigation) · [Data Fetching](https://domphy.com/docs/app/data-fetching) · [Metadata](https://domphy.com/docs/app/metadata) · [Middleware](https://domphy.com/docs/app/middleware) · [SSR](https://domphy.com/docs/app/ssr) · [API Routes](https://domphy.com/docs/app/api-routes) · [Image & Script](https://domphy.com/docs/app/assets) · [i18n Routing](https://domphy.com/docs/app/i18n) · [API Reference](https://domphy.com/docs/app/api)

## i18n (`@domphy/i18n`)

Reactive i18next wrapper for Domphy. `createI18n<TLocale, TMessages>(options)` returns `{ t, locale, currentLocale, exists, setLocale, getLocale, initI18n, detectLocale }`. `locale` is a reactive `State<TLocale>` — read with `(l) => i18n.locale.get(l)` to bind UI to locale changes. `currentLocale(listener)` is sugar for `locale.get(listener)` — reactive locale code in one call. `exists(key)` returns `true` if the key is present in the active locale. Reactive call: `t(listener, key, vars?)` re-renders on `setLocale()`; static: `t(key, vars?)`. Typed keys via generic parameter. Options: `globalKey` (dedup across chunks), `namespace`, `locales` (translation objects keyed by locale), `defaultLocale`, `interpolation` (optional `{ escapeValue?: boolean }` — defaults to `true`, i18next's safe default; pass `false` to disable HTML escaping globally).

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/i18n/) · [Setup](https://domphy.com/docs/i18n/setup) · [Namespaces](https://domphy.com/docs/i18n/namespaces) · [Pluralization](https://domphy.com/docs/i18n/pluralization) · [Interpolation & Formatting](https://domphy.com/docs/i18n/formatting) · [Lazy Loading & Backend](https://domphy.com/docs/i18n/backend) · [Integration Guide](https://domphy.com/docs/i18n/integration) · [API](https://domphy.com/docs/i18n/api)

## Press (`@domphy/press`)

VitePress-baseline static doc site framework. `defineConfig(SiteConfig)` → build with `buildSite` / serve with `startServer` / `startDevServer`. Nested config: `themeConfig.{ nav, sidebar, search, socialLinks, editLink, mermaid, announcementBar, tocTitle, slots }`. Layout elements carry inline `style:{}` objects; `generateCSS()` (Domphy core) emits all layout CSS from those — no class-targeted CSS strings for layout. `pressCSS()` covers only global reset + icon masks + JS-managed state rules. Override any layout region via `themeConfig.slots`. Markdown extras: `<Badge type="tip|info|warning|danger" text="..." />` inline component (self-closing; renders as a styled `dp-badge` span; no JS); 14 container types (tip/warning/info/danger/note/abstract/success/question/failure/bug/example/quote/details/steps); card/card-grid/link-card containers; code-group tabs; line highlighting `{1,3-5}`; `:line-numbers`; `[!code ++/--/highlight/focus/error/warning]` per-line annotations; `<<<` file imports; task lists; mark/sub/sup; emoji; image lazy loading; heading anchors; mermaid CDN. Browser-safe sub-path: `@domphy/press/browser` (layout + routes + theme + search UI, no Node.js built-ins — use in esbuild/Vite client bundles; exports `defineConfig`, `pressCSS`, `flattenSidebar`, `prevNextForRoute`, `sidebarForRoute`, `queryIndex`, `searchWidget`, `mountSearch`, `pageShell`, `homeShell`; types: `SiteConfig`, `UserConfig`, `LayoutContext`, `DomphyElement`, `EditLink`, `IslandRef`, `LayoutSlots`, `LocaleConfig`, `NavItem`, `PageEntry`, `RenderDocOptions`, `RenderedDoc`, `SearchDocument`, `SidebarItem`, `SocialLink`, `ThemeConfig`, `TocEntry`). Exports (main): `buildSite`, `defineConfig`, `startServer`, `startDevServer`, `pressCSS`, `renderDoc`, `discoverPages`, `outFileForRoute`, `routeForFile`, `sidebarForRoute`, `prevNextForRoute`, `flattenSidebar`, `buildSearchIndex`, `queryIndex`, `searchWidget`, `mountSearch`, `createHighlighter`, `parseFenceInfo`, `renderFence`, `pageShell`, `homeShell`; types: `SiteConfig`, `UserConfig`, `ThemeConfig`, `LayoutSlots`, `LayoutContext`, `DomphyElement`, `EditLink`, `IslandRef`, `LocaleConfig`, `NavItem`, `PageEntry`, `RenderDocOptions`, `RenderedDoc`, `SearchDocument`, `SidebarItem`, `SocialLink`, `TocEntry`, `FenceMeta`, `SearchResult`, `SearchWidgetOptions`.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/press/) · [Config](https://domphy.com/docs/press/config) · [Routing](https://domphy.com/docs/press/routing) · [Markdown](https://domphy.com/docs/press/markdown) · [Extending Markdown](https://domphy.com/docs/press/extending-markdown) · [Search](https://domphy.com/docs/press/search) · [i18n](https://domphy.com/docs/press/i18n) · [Custom Theme & Slots](https://domphy.com/docs/press/custom-theme) · [Customization](https://domphy.com/docs/press/customization) · [Deploy](https://domphy.com/docs/press/deploy) · [API](https://domphy.com/docs/press/api)

## Markdown (`@domphy/markdown`)

Parse Markdown into Domphy element trees for SSR/SSG — `parseMarkdown(md, options)` → `{ frontmatter, body, toc }` (uses remark/unified + mdast under the hood; pluggable `highlight`, anchors, TOC). GFM enabled by default. **Plugin API:** `createMarkdown(options?)` creates a reusable `MarkdownInstance` (`{ parse, toDomphy }`) with a shared remark processor; accepts `plugins: RemarkPlugin[]` (unified Plugin array), `math: boolean` (shorthand for `remark-math`; must install separately; preserves LaTeX as text for CDN KaTeX/MathJax auto-render), `onCustom(node, helper)` (handle unrecognised mdast node types). Lower-level exports: `walkMdast(root, options?)` — convert an mdast Root to DomphyElement[]; `splitFrontmatter(md)` — extract YAML frontmatter → `{ frontmatter, content }`; `markdownToDomphy(md, options?)` — convenience wrapper returning only `DomphyElement[]`; `createUniqueSlugger(slugify)` / `defaultSlugify` — slug utilities. Types: `FrontmatterSplit`, `CreateMarkdownOptions`, `MarkdownInstance`, `RemarkPlugin`, `AnchorSlugify`, `Highlight`, `ParseOptions`, `ParseResult`, `TocEntry`, `MdastWalkOptions`, `WalkHelper`.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/markdown/) · [Frontmatter](https://domphy.com/docs/markdown/frontmatter) · [Syntax Reference](https://domphy.com/docs/markdown/syntax-reference) · [Syntax Highlighting](https://domphy.com/docs/markdown/highlighting) · [Table of Contents](https://domphy.com/docs/markdown/table-of-contents)

## Mermaid (`@domphy/mermaid`)

Render Mermaid diagrams for Domphy — build-time `renderMermaidToSvg` / `renderMermaidInTree` (replaces ```mermaid code blocks with inline SVG via mermaid-cli) and a client `mermaidClient()` patch. Also exported: `renderMermaidCached(source, options?)` (wraps `renderMermaidToSvg` with on-disk SVG caching), `normalizeMermaidSource(source)` (normalizes whitespace/newlines before hashing and rendering), `cacheKey(source)` (stable hash used by the on-disk cache), `DEFAULT_CACHE_DIR` constant. Types: `MermaidBrowserModule`, `MermaidClientOptions`, `MermaidLoader`, `CacheOptions`, `MermaidOptions`, `MermaidRenderer`, `MermaidTheme` (`"default" | "dark" | "neutral" | "forest" | "base"`), `TreeOptions`.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/) · [Supported Diagrams](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/diagrams) · [Configuration & Themes](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/configuration) · [Flowchart](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/flowchart) · [Sequence Diagrams](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/sequence) · [Class & State Diagrams](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/class-diagram) · [ER, Gantt, Pie & Git Graphs](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/er-gantt-pie) · [Client-Side Rendering](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/client) · [Build-Time Integration](https://domphy.com/docs/mermaid/build)

## Palette (`@domphy/palette`)

Color-palette quality engine (design-time companion to `@domphy/theme`): measure palette quality with 5 CIELAB metrics (`Ramp`, `Palette`, `Swatch`). Zero runtime dependencies, pure color science.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/palette/) · [Measuring quality](https://domphy.com/docs/palette/measuring) · [Ramp](https://domphy.com/docs/palette/ramp) · [Accessibility & Contrast](https://domphy.com/docs/palette/accessibility) · [Swatch & Color Utilities](https://domphy.com/docs/palette/swatch)

**Color-space utilities** (all named exports): `hexToRgb` / `rgbToHex` — hex ↔ linear sRGB; `rgbToLab` / `labToRgb` — CIELAB (D65); `rgbToOklab` / `oklabToRgb` — Oklab (Björn Ottosson 2020); `labToLch` / `lchToLab` — polar LCH; `toLightnessEAL` / `fromLightnessEAL` — Equivalent Achromatic Lightness (High et al. 2023, Helmholtz–Kohlrausch correction); `calcDeltaE2000` — CIEDE2000 perceptual distance; `cssRgbToRgb` — parse CSS `rgb()` string.

**A11y, mixing, gradient**: `contrastRatio(hex1, hex2)` → WCAG 2.1 contrast ratio (≥4.5 = AA, ≥7 = AAA); `mix(hex1, hex2, ratio=0.5, space='oklab')` → blend two colors perceptually (`'oklab'`|`'lab'`|`'rgb'`); `scale(colors, steps)` → N-step Oklab gradient across ≥2 anchors, returns hex array.

**Quality analysis**: `new Ramp(hexes, name)` → `.metrics` (5 CIELAB scores 0–1), `.score` (0–100), `.wcag[45].span`, `.apca[60].span`; `new Palette(colorsRecord, name)` → aggregate metrics via RMS; `new Swatch(hex)` → `.lab`, `.lch`, `.lightness` (L_EAL), `.chroma`, `.hue`, `.luminance`.

## Audit (`@domphy/audit`)

Two complementary audit modes:

**Static element-tree a11y** (zero-dep, no browser): `auditA11y(element)` checks a Domphy element tree for WCAG-aligned a11y violations and returns `{ ok, issues }` synchronously. Rules: `missing-alt` (img without alt), `missing-label` (input/textarea/select without accessible name via aria-label / aria-labelledby / label[for] / nesting / title), `heading-hierarchy` (skipped heading level, e.g. h1→h3), `missing-lang` (html without lang attribute). `A11yIssue` has `{ rule, message, path }` (no rect — no browser coords). Works in Node, Vitest, at build time.

**Runtime layout checks via Playwright**: `scanInteractive(page)` runs static + interactive checks — auto-discovers nav/dropdown triggers, hovers each, checks for overlay bugs. Returns `{ ok, issues, svg }`.

Playwright issue types:
- **theme** — `checkTheme(page)`: missing `data-theme` on `<html>`, unresolved `--neutral-0` CSS var (transparent backgrounds)
- **overlay** — `checkOverlays(page)`: visible positioned overlays with transparent backgrounds or hover gap >4px from offset parent
- **overlap** — `detectOverlaps(page)`: sibling elements whose bounding boxes intersect
- **geometry** — `verifyGeometry(page)`: Domphy button height formula `(6+2d)*U`
- **contrast** — `checkContrast(page)`: WCAG 4.5:1 on text elements

CLI: `npx @domphy/audit <url> [--static]` — runs full `scanInteractive` and exits 1 if issues found. Requires `playwright` as a peer dep.

`AuditPageFull` extends `AuditPage` with `hover(selector)` + `waitForTimeout(ms)` — compatible with `playwright.Page` directly.

`svg` output: lightweight SVG skeleton annotated with issue positions (red=overlap/theme/overlay, orange=geometry, gold=contrast). Individual checkers importable separately. No dep on `@domphy/core`.

`checkLayout(page, options?)` — static-only aggregator (no hover discovery): runs all five checkers concurrently via `Promise.all` and returns `{ ok, issues, svg }`. `options.checks` selects which checkers run (default: all five); `options.tolerance` tunes geometry tolerance (default 1px); `options.minContrast` tunes contrast threshold (default 4.5). Faster than `scanInteractive` when interactive hover discovery is not needed. Also exported: `snapshot(page)` (captures element layout rects into a `LayoutSnapshot`), `toSVG(layout, issues)` (produces the annotated SVG skeleton), `LayoutSnapshot` type.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/audit/) · [Static a11y](https://domphy.com/docs/audit/a11y) · [Interpreting Audit Results](https://domphy.com/docs/audit/interpreting-results) · [CI Integration](https://domphy.com/docs/audit/ci-integration) · [checkLayout API](https://domphy.com/docs/audit/check-layout)

## Doctor (`@domphy/doctor`)

Static analyzer for Domphy element trees — `diagnose(element, opts?)` returns `Diagnostic[]`; `validate(element, opts?)` returns `{ ok, issues, summary }`; `format(diagnostics)` formats a `Diagnostic[]` into a human-readable string; `fix(element)` returns `{ tree, applied, report }`, applying lossless fixes (e.g. void-content) and listing the rest. 18 built-in rules: `inline-typography`, `raw-theme-value` (hex/rgb AND CSS named colors like "red"/"white" on direct color props), `raw-spacing-value` (incl. logical props paddingBlock/paddingInline/marginBlock/marginInline), `unknown-tone` (dataTone grammar + range ≤ 17), `middle-surface-anchor` (dataTone shift-4–13 collapses contrast), `tone-background-inherit` (backgroundColor must be themeColor(l, "inherit") — shift the surface via dataTone instead), `low-contrast` (text `color` and `backgroundColor` are both reactive theme vars but their shift numbers differ by < 9 — insufficient contrast; the shift N is extracted from the `var(--X-N)` string that `themeColor()` returns), `low-opacity` (style.opacity < 0.6 on interactive controls is too dim; warns warning or info if hover-restore pattern detected), `missing-color` (element uses themeColor for at least one styled prop but has no `style.color` — text color won't re-evaluate when the tone context shifts; CSS `color` inheritance carries the computed value from the parent, not a live theme var), `dataTone-surface-contract` (element sets dataTone but is missing `backgroundColor` and/or `color` — a tone context surface must declare both so children can guarantee readable contrast), `color-shift-minimum` (element with dataTone sets `style.color` to a tone step < 9 — below the minimum required for legible text on a standard surface), `unknown-density` (dataDensity range ≤ 4), `unknown-size` (dataSize range ≤ 7), `void-content`, `missing-key`, `duplicate-key`, `unstable-key`, `unknown-tag`. `DiagnoseOptions`: `runReactive` (default true), `only: string[]` (run only listed rules), `exclude: string[]` (skip listed rules), `rules: CustomRule[]` (project-specific extension rules). Inline suppression: add `_doctorDisable: true | "rule-id" | string[]` to any element to suppress rules at that node. `Diagnostic` carries optional `category` field: "structure" | "key" | "theme" | "typography" | "data-attr" | "visual". Run it on generated code and fix what it reports — the AI self-correction loop. Pairs with the app-block registry (`app-manifest.json`) for large codebases.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/doctor/) · [Rules Reference](https://domphy.com/docs/doctor/rules) · [Configuration & API](https://domphy.com/docs/doctor/configuration) · [Agent Loop](https://domphy.com/docs/doctor/agent-loop)

## Floating (`@domphy/floating`)

Anchor positioning for tooltips, popovers, dropdowns, and menus — a 1-1 vendor of [floating-ui](https://github.com/floating-ui/floating-ui) (`@floating-ui/dom` + core + utils) bundled as a **zero-dependency** package. Used internally by `@domphy/ui` overlays (`tooltip`, `popover`, `selectBox`, `combobox`). Full [Floating UI reference](https://floating-ui.com) applies as-is.

Also exports `createFloating(config?)` — a stateful Popper.js-equivalent manager that wraps `computePosition` + `autoUpdate` into a reusable handle: `handle.connect(reference, floating, options?)` starts auto-update; `handle.disconnect()` stops it; `handle.onUpdate(callback)` subscribes to position changes; `handle.position` holds the last computed `{ x, y, placement, strategy, middlewareData }` (null before first update). Types: `FloatingHandle`, `FloatingPosition`.

- [Getting Started](https://domphy.com/docs/floating) · [Placement](https://domphy.com/docs/floating/placement) · [offset](https://domphy.com/docs/floating/offset) · [flip & autoPlacement](https://domphy.com/docs/floating/flip) · [shift & limitShift](https://domphy.com/docs/floating/shift) · [arrow](https://domphy.com/docs/floating/arrow) · [size](https://domphy.com/docs/floating/size) · [autoUpdate, hide, Virtual Elements & createFloating](https://domphy.com/docs/floating/auto-update)

## Chart (`@domphy/chart`)

ECharts-grade data visualization for Domphy — two-layer rendering: **luma.gl v9 WebGL** for series geometry (bar, line, scatter, pie, radar, heatmap, candlestick) + **SVG overlay** for axes, grid lines, tick labels, title, legend, tooltip, mark points/lines/areas.

### Usage

```ts
import { chart } from "@domphy/chart";

{ div: null, $: [chart(option)] }
// or with reactive option:
const optionState = toState({ type: "line", ... });
{ div: null, $: [chart(optionState)] }
```

### ChartEngine (advanced embedding)

```ts
import { ChartEngine } from "@domphy/chart";
const engine = new ChartEngine(container);
await engine.init();
engine.setSize(width, height);
engine.setOption(option);
engine.destroy();
```

### API surface (ECharts grade)

- **series (WebGL)**: `line` (smooth, step, areaStyle, connectNulls, stack, gradient area via `GradientObject`), `bar` (stack, grouped, horizontal — swap to `yAxis: "category"` + `xAxis: "value"`), `scatter` (symbolSize fn), `pie` (radius, roseType, center), `radar`, `heatmap`, `candlestick` (upColor/downColor), `gauge` (SVG, progress arc, ticks, labels)
- **series (SVG)**: `boxplot` (min/Q1/median/Q3/max with whiskers), `funnel` (sorted trapezoids with labels), `treemap` (squarified layout, recursive children, auto labels), `sankey` (BFS depth + flow relaxation, bezier links), `graph` (Fruchterman-Reingold force-directed, arrows, categories)
- **series labels**: `label: { show: true, position: "top"|"inside"|"bottom", formatter, fontSize }` on bar, line, scatter, pie (with leader lines + percentage)
- **axes**: `xAxis`/`yAxis` — type `"value" | "category" | "time" | "log"`, `name`, `min`/`max`, `splitNumber`, `gridIndex`, `axisLabel.rotate`
- **grid**: `left`/`top`/`right`/`bottom` (px or %)
- **polar**: `center`, `radius`
- **radar**: `indicator[]`, `shape`, `splitNumber`, `startAngle`
- **title**: `text`, `subtext`, `left`/`top`
- **legend**: `orient`, `data[]`, `left`/`top`/`right`/`bottom` — **click to show/hide series** (interactive)
- **tooltip**: `trigger` (`"axis"` for line/bar — all series at x; `"item"` for pie/scatter — hit-test nearest sector/point), `axisPointer.type`, `formatter`
- **dataset**: `source`, `dimensions`, `transform[]` (filter/sort)
- **markPoint/markLine/markArea**: `max`/`min`/`average` special values, `coord`, `xAxis`/`yAxis`
- **dataZoom**: slider (drag handles + center pan) + inside (mouse wheel); clips domain on xAxis; `start`/`end` (0–100%)
- **visualMap**: continuous (gradient bar, min/max labels) + piecewise (color + label per piece); `colorFromVisualMap(vm, value)` utility
- **toolbox**: type definitions (not yet rendered)
- **brush**: type definitions (not yet rendered)

### Gradient area fills

`areaStyle.color` accepts a `GradientObject` for gradient fills:

```ts
areaStyle: {
  color: {
    type: "linear", x: 0, y: 0, x2: 0, y2: 1,
    colorStops: [
      { offset: 0, color: "rgba(58,77,233,0.8)" },
      { offset: 1, color: "rgba(58,77,233,0.05)" },
    ],
  },
}
```

Types: `LinearGradient` (`type:"linear"`, x/y/x2/y2, colorStops), `RadialGradient` (`type:"radial"`, x/y/r, colorStops), `GradientObject = LinearGradient | RadialGradient`, `ColorStop { offset, color }`. All exported from `@domphy/chart`.

### Color system

Series palette cycles through theme families (`primary → secondary → success → warning → error → info → highlight → attention → danger`) at tone `shift-9`. All WebGL uniforms use `themeColorToken(null, tone, family)` → concrete hex (no CSS var, light-theme default). Override any series color via `series[n].color: ThemeFamily`.

### Scale utilities (also exported)

```ts
import { createLinearScale, createOrdinalScale, createTimeScale, createLogScale } from "@domphy/chart";
```

### Dataset utilities (also exported)

```ts
import { applyTransforms, resolveDataset } from "@domphy/chart";
```

- `applyTransforms(source, transforms)` — applies a `TransformOption[]` pipeline (filter / sort) to a row array. Compatible with `DatasetOption.transform`.
- `resolveDataset(dataset)` — resolves a `DatasetOption` (handles array-of-arrays with header row, column-object format, and inline transforms) into a normalized row array.

### Color utilities (also exported)

```ts
import { hexToRgba, seriesHex, seriesRgba, familyHex, familyRgba, seriesPaletteFamily } from "@domphy/chart";
// type Rgba = [r: number, g: number, b: number, a: number]  (floats 0–1)
```

- `hexToRgba(hex, alpha?)` → `Rgba` — converts `"#rrggbb"` / `"#rrggbbaa"` to a float-tuple.
- `seriesHex(index)` / `seriesRgba(index, alpha?)` — concrete color for the nth series (cycles through the 9-family palette at `shift-9`).
- `familyHex(family, tone?)` / `familyRgba(family, tone?, alpha?)` — color for a `ThemeFamily` name at an optional tone (default `shift-9`).
- `seriesPaletteFamily(index)` → `ThemeFamily` — maps a series index to its theme family name (`"primary"`, `"secondary"`, …).
- Also exported: `ZoomWindow` type (bounding box returned by dataZoom coord utilities), `Rgba` type.

- [Overview](https://domphy.com/docs/chart/) · [Series Reference](https://domphy.com/docs/chart/series) · [Axes & Grid](https://domphy.com/docs/chart/axes) · [Colors & Theme](https://domphy.com/docs/chart/colors) · [ChartEngine API](https://domphy.com/docs/chart/engine)

## Scaffolder (`create-domphy`)

`npm create domphy@latest <dir>` — scaffolds a runnable Vite + TypeScript starter: `themeApply`, sample patches, and an `AGENTS.md` ready for AI agents. Not scoped as `@domphy/*`; published separately.

## AI tooling

- [`manifest.json`](https://domphy.com/manifest.json): machine-readable index of every package + patch (name, host tag, signature, props schema, doc, example). Query this instead of parsing docs.
- [`tones.json`](https://domphy.com/tones.json): valid tone names + theme color names for `themeColor()` / `dataTone`.
- [`@domphy/mcp`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@domphy/mcp): MCP server. Tools: `domphy_list_patches`, `domphy_get_patch`, `domphy_list_packages`, `domphy_rules`, `domphy_tones`, `domphy_diagnose`, `domphy_validate`, `domphy_fix`, `domphy_list_app_blocks`, `domphy_get_app_block`.
- [Full text dump for one-shot context](https://domphy.com/llms-full.txt): rules + quickstart + every core/theme/package doc + every patch source. Use when you need the whole framework in a single fetch.
- [Building with AI](https://domphy.com/docs/ai): per-tool setup.
- [Playground](https://domphy.com/docs/playground): interactive sandbox
- [GitHub](https://github.com/domphy/domphy): source, issues, releases
